Categorized technology portfolio: podded propulsion (5 MW class), four-pod interaction, CLT propellers, transom reference, stern conditioning, underbody air-retention, air lubrication, coatings/riblet
R_Int ∝ f(t_root, C_root), largely independent of viscous friction, less subject to scale effects, critical penalty in multi-pod configurations
Wedge-shaped fins on hull aft sections, jBAY Source Term Model implementation in OpenFOAM, 7.5% drag reduction validated on bulk carrier at model scale
Bio-inspired microscopic longitudinal ridges, 3-4% drag reduction in lab, but extreme localized shear stress spikes (880 Pa vs. 130 Pa average), fouling risk, durability unproven at scale
Bubble DR 2-5%, air layer 8-14%, air cavity 16-22% in calm water. Weather degrades savings 15-35%. Cavity length ∝ U²/g, limited to ~0.5λ
Standard for scale extrapolation, decomposes total resistance into frictional and form components, mandates explicit correlation allowance for surface roughness

nodes.l: Hull Vane vs. Stern Flap Contradiction
nodes.x: Interceptors suppress wave resistance without wetted surface penalty; Hull Vane adds wetted surface frictional penalty. At low Froude (<0.20), interceptor wins; at high Froude (>0.25), Hull Vane lift
Wet (FrT<1.5), transitional (1.5-2.2), ventilated (FrT>2.2). Critical thresholds: model scale FrT*≈2.5, full-scale FrT*≈2.0. Transom contributes 50% of total drag at model scale, 70% at full scale

Formalized learning loop tracking divergence between reduced-order Michell prediction and OpenFOAM authority CFD. Error classes: Expected, Topology-Missed, Package-Overcredit, Interaction-Missed, Nume
Reduced-order lane (Michell, empirical forms, seconds execution) + authority lane (OpenFOAM RANS, hours execution). Governs interplay via Get Physics Done (GPD) runtime
bound4blue eSAILs or OceanWings, 500-1,500 kW average at 23 kn (3-10%), 700-2,000 kW at 18 kn (10-28%). Hydrogen savings material: 1.44 t/day at 18 kn with 1 MW wind = 26% storage reduction

Conservative (old priors) 20.06 MW, mid-case refreshed 16.4 MW, aggressive 15.6 MW, irreducible floor 14.5 MW. 20 MW baseline chosen: covers conservative band with N-1 redundancy at 15 MW
700 bar compressed impractical (1,222 t tankage, 2,115 m³); liquid H₂ is only viable option at 55 tonnes (350 t system, 1,150 m³). 70% mass reduction, 45% volume reduction
225 kW, ~60% efficiency, ~700 kg mass, LR certified. 20 MW plant requires ~89 modules. Largest marine module currently available at scale

Geometry-first computational manifold with contractive fixed-point dynamics. Validates through 236 tests on heterogeneous hardware (M1, Snapdragon 8 Gen 2). Foundation substrate for deterministic comp
DM3 manifold satisfies Banach contraction mapping theorem: spectral radius ρ(J) ≤ 164/165, guaranteeing unique fixed-point convergence. Core mathematical guarantee of DM3.
Monotonic energy descent property: ΔE ≤ 0 per iteration. Lyapunov-like function guarantees the manifold settles to stable states without oscillation.
Boundary conditions encode interior state with ≥0.99 cosine similarity. Enables efficient representation: boundary ↔ bulk information transfer without full interior reconstruction.
Soliton-like spectral dynamics with quantized energy invariants. Stable propagating structures in the manifold that carry quantized information.
Computational pattern in DM3: Arnold tongues emerge showing mode-locking between frequencies. Enables frequency-selective computation.
DM3 supports quasicrystal-like pattern formation in its state space. Aperiodic tiling with long-range order.
DM3 manifold exhibits Faraday wave instabilities: parametrically driven pattern formation at subharmonic frequencies.
DM3 preserves topological invariants under continuous deformation. Gauge-like holonomy under parameter loops.

236 empirical validators testing DM3 properties. All pass on first run across heterogeneous hardware (Apple M1, Snapdragon 8 Gen 2). Covers: contraction, energy descent, holography, solitons, mode-loc
Major DM3 research initiative: RM10 bridge qualification as primary computational platform. Active hypothesis investigation.
DM3 validator tuning workstream: repairing F1/F2 root surface fidelity to restore manifold accuracy.
DM3 heterogeneous bootstrap via resonance chambers. Technique for initializing the manifold state using resonance-based coupling.
Scientific reconstruction method using Meru geometry for fidelity recovery in DM3. Leverages geometric structure for accurate state reconstruction.
Heterogeneous capability discovery: DM3 acts as a computational resource ('battery') that can be allocated across different workloads based on manifold capacity.
Foundational document: geometry precedes computation. All DM3 structures derive from geometric first principles rather than algorithmic construction.
DM3 System-on-Chip deployment policy. Defines how the manifold maps to hardware-specific execution on constrained devices (mobile SoCs).
Registry of DM3 tests validated on specific SoC hardware. Maps validator IDs to chip families and pass/fail evidence.
